text: Adobe's stock has experienced notable volatility in recent trading sessions, with shares declining amid broader market concerns about AI competition in the creative software space. According to market commentary, Adobe stock fell approximately 1.7% following Google's announcement of enhanced image editing capabilities in its Gemini app, which investors viewed as potentially threatening Adobe's dominance in digital imaging C018. The stock closed at $354.91 in one recent session, representing a -2.29% shift from the previous trading day C014. Year-to-date, Adobe shares have lost 17.8% as investors remain skeptical about the company's AI product monetization strategy C015. Over the past six months, shareholders have lost 18.6% of their capital, underperforming the S&P 500 which climbed 8.8% during the same period C006.
